title: Australia Faces First Mainland Case of Deadly Bird Flu
subtitle: Authorities work to contain the virus after a migratory bird tests positive in Western Australia

summary.en: Australia has officially confirmed its first mainland case of the H5N1 bird flu virus. A migratory brown skua found in a Western Australian national park tested positive for the disease. While the virus has caused massive damage to wildlife populations across the globe, this is the first time it has reached the Australian mainland. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese described the situation as concerning but noted that the government has spent millions on preparation. Officials are now working to determine if the virus has spread to other local wildlife.
summary.zh: 澳洲正式確認境內首起 H5N1 禽流感病例，一隻在西澳州國家公園發現的遷徙褐賊鷗檢測呈陽性。儘管該病毒已在全球對野生動物族群造成巨大破壞，但這是首次傳入澳洲本土。總理艾班尼斯形容情況令人擔憂，但指出政府已投入數百萬資金進行準備。官員目前正致力於釐清病毒是否已傳播至其他當地野生動物。

paragraphs.en: Australia has officially confirmed its first mainland case of the highly dangerous H5N1 bird flu. This virus, which has caused the deaths of millions of birds and various mammals around the world, was found in a brown skua at Cape Le Grand national park in Western Australia. According to The Guardian, the bird was found unwell last Sunday and later died from the disease. This discovery marks a significant moment for the country, as Australia was previously the only continent that had avoided the spread of this specific virus.
paragraphs.zh: 澳洲已正式確認境內首起高危險性 H5N1 禽流感病例。這種病毒已在全球導致數百萬隻鳥類及多種哺乳動物死亡，此次是在西澳州勒格蘭角國家公園（Cape Le Grand National Park）的一隻褐賊鷗身上發現。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，這隻鳥在上週日被發現時狀況不佳，隨後因病死亡。這項發現對澳洲而言是一個重大時刻，因為澳洲此前是唯一未受該特定病毒傳播影響的大陸。

paragraphs.en: Agriculture Minister Julie Collins confirmed the news, noting that the government had long expected the virus to arrive eventually. "We all knew that we couldn’t be bird flu-free forever," Collins said during a media conference. While the virus was previously found on remote Australian territories near the Antarctic in October last year, this is the first time it has been detected on the mainland. Tests are currently being conducted on a second bird, a giant petrel found in the same area, to see if it was also infected with the disease.
paragraphs.zh: 農業部長柯林斯（Julie Collins）證實了這項消息，並指出政府早已預料到病毒終將傳入。柯林斯在媒體記者會上表示：「我們都知道不可能永遠免於禽流感。」雖然去年十月在南極附近的澳洲偏遠領地曾發現過該病毒，但這是首次在澳洲本土偵測到。目前當局正對在同一地區發現的另一隻巨海燕進行檢測，以確認其是否也感染了該疾病。

paragraphs.en: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese described the arrival of the virus as "concerning" but emphasized that the government has been preparing for this event for a long time. The government has already spent 113 million dollars on plans to manage the situation. "What we’re about is making sure, firstly, that we do whatever we can to restrict the spread," the prime minister stated. He explained that the virus arrived through migratory birds, which travel long distances across the globe, making it difficult to prevent the disease from reaching new areas.
paragraphs.zh: 總理艾班尼斯（Anthony Albanese）形容病毒的到來「令人擔憂」，但強調政府早已為此做好長期準備。政府已投入 1.13 億澳元用於管理相關局勢的計畫。總理表示：「我們首要目標是盡一切努力限制病毒擴散。」他解釋說，病毒是透過在全球長距離遷徙的候鳥傳入，這使得防止疾病進入新區域變得相當困難。

paragraphs.en: Experts are now working to understand how far the virus has traveled. Chief veterinary officer Beth Cookson explained that the Western Australian government is conducting on-ground surveillance—a process of watching and checking an area carefully—to see if the disease is present in native bird populations. "At this early stage, it is difficult to make a conclusion about whether the infection has established in wildlife populations," Cookson said. She added that officials are trying to find out if the virus is only in a few migratory birds or if it has already started to spread among local animals.
paragraphs.zh: 專家們正致力於了解病毒傳播的範圍。首席獸醫官庫克森（Beth Cookson）解釋說，西澳州政府正在進行地面監測——即仔細觀察並檢查特定區域的過程——以確認當地鳥類族群是否出現疫情。庫克森表示：「在現階段，很難斷定感染是否已在野生動物族群中紮根。」她補充說，官員們正試圖釐清病毒是僅存在於少數候鳥身上，還是已經開始在當地動物間傳播。

paragraphs.en: For now, the government reports that there is no evidence of mass deaths in wildlife or any infections within the country’s poultry or agriculture systems. Poultry refers to birds raised for food, such as chickens and ducks. Cookson noted that while the news is worrying for many Australians who care deeply about the country’s unique wildlife, the nation is as prepared as it can be. Australia has been able to learn from the experiences of other countries that have already fought the virus.
paragraphs.zh: 目前政府報告指出，尚無證據顯示野生動物出現大規模死亡，國內的家禽或農業系統也未受感染。家禽是指為了食用而飼養的鳥類，例如雞和鴨。庫克森指出，雖然這則消息對許多深切關心澳洲獨特野生動物的民眾來說令人擔憂，但國家已做好充分準備。澳洲得以借鏡其他已與該病毒對抗過的國家之經驗。

paragraphs.en: Conservation groups have expressed deep concern about what this means for the future. Kate Millar, the chief executive of BirdLife Australia, said that the potential impact on native wildlife could be very serious. "This virus has devastated wildlife populations overseas. This could be the beginning of a long fight to protect birds and wildlife in Australia," Millar warned. She noted that the damage caused by H5N1 in other parts of the world has been extreme, and the arrival of the virus could change the environment for many native species.
paragraphs.zh: 保育團體對此發展對未來的影響表示深切擔憂。澳洲鳥類協會（BirdLife Australia）執行長米勒（Kate Millar）表示，對原生野生動物的潛在衝擊可能非常嚴重。米勒警告：「這種病毒已在海外重創野生動物族群。這可能是保護澳洲鳥類與野生動物長期抗戰的開端。」她指出，H5N1 在世界其他地區造成的破壞極為嚴重，病毒的到來可能會改變許多原生物種的生存環境。

paragraphs.en: As the situation continues to develop, authorities have issued clear advice to the public. Officials are asking people to avoid any contact with sick or dead birds. If a member of the public sees a bird that looks unwell or has died, they should not touch it. Instead, they are encouraged to take a photograph and report the exact location to the local authorities. This information helps experts track the movement of the virus and respond more effectively.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著情勢持續發展，當局已向大眾發布明確建議。官員呼籲民眾避免與生病或死亡的鳥類接觸。若民眾發現鳥類看起來不健康或已經死亡，請勿觸摸。相反地，建議民眾拍照並向當地主管機關回報確切位置。這些資訊有助於專家追蹤病毒動向並更有效地做出回應。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the government plans to continue its coordinated response across different states and territories. A meeting was held on Saturday to discuss the next steps in the investigation. The main goal for the coming days is to determine if the virus is just passing through with migratory birds or if it has truly established itself within the Australian ecosystem. As the nation faces this new challenge, the focus remains on limiting the spread and protecting the country's diverse and fragile bird populations from further harm.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，政府計畫持續在各州與領地之間進行協調應對。週六已召開會議討論調查的後續步驟。未來幾天的主要目標是釐清病毒只是隨著候鳥過境，還是已經真正進入澳洲生態系統。隨著國家面臨這項新挑戰，重點仍放在限制病毒擴散，並保護國內多樣且脆弱的鳥類族群免受進一步傷害。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Focused on or concerned with a particular goal or priority.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作介系詞（關於），這裡作形容詞，表示政府當前的首要任務。文中：What we’re about is making sure, firstly, that we do whatever we can to restrict the spread.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become accepted or settled in a new environment or place.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （疾病或物種）在某地落地生根、定居。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（建立），這裡指病毒在野生動物群體中紮根。文中：At this early stage, it is difficult to make a conclusion about whether the infection has established in wildlife populations.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be in a place for a short time before moving on to another destination.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思容易誤解為「通過」，這裡指病毒僅隨候鳥短暫經過，未長期停留。文中：The main goal for the coming days is to determine if the virus is just passing through with migratory birds or if it has truly established itself within the Australian ecosystem.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Anthony Albanese says Australia’s first mainland case of deadly H5N1 bird flu ‘concerning’

previousArticles.title: Australia Confirms First Mainland Case of H5N1 Bird Flu
previousArticles.summaryEn: Australia has officially confirmed its first mainland case of the H5N1 bird flu virus. The disease was found in a brown skua at a national park in Western Australia. While the virus has caused significant harm to wildlife globally, officials report no signs of mass deaths in the country yet. The government has invested millions in preparation and is currently monitoring the situation closely. Authorities are urging the public to avoid contact with sick or dead birds.

quiz.question: Where exactly was the first case of H5N1 bird flu detected on the Australian mainland?

options.A: At a remote territory near the Antarctic
options.B: At Cape Le Grand national park
options.D: In a laboratory in Western Australia

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段，病毒是在西澳的 Cape Le Grand 國家公園的一隻褐賊鷗（brown skua）身上發現的。選項 A 錯誤，因為那是去年十月在偏遠領地發現的，而非本次的「大陸」首例。選項 C 錯誤，文章第五段明確指出目前農業系統（家禽）尚未發現感染。選項 D 雖然提到測試在進行，但發現地點並非實驗室。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what can be inferred about the government's current strategy regarding the virus?

options.A: They believe they can completely eradicate the virus from the continent immediately.
options.B: They are prioritizing the protection of international trade over wildlife conservation.
options.C: They are focusing on monitoring and containment rather than claiming the virus is already under control.
options.D: They were caught off guard by the arrival of the virus despite their financial investment.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。從第四段與第八段可知，專家目前仍在進行監控（surveillance），試圖釐清病毒是僅隨候鳥經過還是已在當地生態系紮根，且總理強調重點在於「限制傳播」。選項 A 太過樂觀，文章並未提到能立即根除。選項 B 錯誤，文章強調的是對野生動物的保護。選項 D 錯誤，第二段與第三段均提到政府早已預期病毒會到來並為此準備許久。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'established' mean as it is used in the phrase 'the infection has established in wildlife populations'?

options.A: Created a formal organization or business
options.B: Become permanently settled and reproducing within a group
options.C: Proven to be true through scientific testing
options.D: Moved from one location to another

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「established」指病毒已在當地野生動物群體中落地生根、持續存在並傳播。選項 A 是該字在商業語境下的常見意思，但在這裡不適用。選項 C 雖然文章提到測試，但「established」本身不等於「被證實」。選項 D 描述的是移動過程，而非「紮根」的狀態。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of this article?

options.A: To provide a scientific guide on how to treat bird flu in mammals.
options.B: To blame migratory birds for the destruction of Australia's environment.
options.C: To report on the first mainland case of H5N1 and the nation's cautious response.
options.D: To argue that the government has failed to protect the country from the H5N1 virus.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文主要報導了澳洲大陸首例 H5N1 禽流感的發現，並詳細說明了政府的監控措施、專家看法以及公眾應對建議，屬於客觀的新聞報導。選項 A 錯誤，文章並未提供治療指南。選項 B 錯誤，文章僅提到候鳥是傳播途徑，並未進行指責。選項 D 錯誤，文章強調政府已投入資源並進行長期準備，並未採取批評立場。
